title: anisotome aromatica var. incisa
intro: Anisotome aromatica var. incisa, commonly known as the New Zealand aniseed, is a plant species that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae) and is native to New Zealand. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it a popular choice for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Anisotome aromatica var. incisa include its ability to alleviate digestive problems, reduce inflammation, and act as an expectorant, making it a potential treatment for respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Anisotome aromatica var. incisa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and roots in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
